title: acosta resupinata subsp. spachii
intro: Acosta resupinata subsp. spachii, also known as Spach's Acosta, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Mexico and is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihemorrhagic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and burns. Acosta resupinata subsp. spachii is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
